author: Nadia Petrov
to: Senior Leadership Team — Operations, Finance, and Technology
subject: Proposal to Consolidate Atlanta and Charlotte Distribution Centers
executive_summary: This memo presents a recommendation to consolidate the Atlanta and Charlotte distribution centers into a single expanded facility in Greenville, South Carolina. The consolidation is projected to reduce annual operating costs by $2.8 million while improving average delivery times to Southeast customers by 0.4 days.
background: The Atlanta facility (opened 2014, 85,000 sq ft) and Charlotte facility (opened 2017, 62,000 sq ft) currently operate at 58% and 43% capacity respectively. Following the shift to direct-to-consumer fulfillment in 2024, throughput requirements have changed significantly. A site evaluation conducted by Prologis in January 2026 identified a 160,000 sq ft facility in Greenville with superior interstate access and a labor cost index 14% below our current blended average.
analysis:
1. Combined rent savings of $1.1M annually (Greenville rate: $6.20/sq ft vs. blended current: $9.85/sq ft)
2. Workforce reduction of 22 redundant positions through automation and process consolidation, with 15 positions offered relocation packages
3. Improved routing efficiency — Greenville sits within 4 hours of 78% of our Southeast customer base vs. 64% for Atlanta alone
4. One-time transition costs estimated at $3.4M (lease termination, equipment relocation, hiring, and IT infrastructure)
5. Break-even timeline: 14 months from operational start
6. Risk factors: 90-day overlap period required to maintain service levels during transition; Charlotte lease early termination penalty of $420,000
recommendation: Proceed with the Greenville consolidation on a 6-month timeline. Sign a letter of intent for the Greenville facility by March 31, begin Charlotte wind-down in Q2, and target full Greenville operations by September 1, 2026. Establish a transition steering committee led by VP of Operations with weekly status reviews.
next_steps:
1. Board briefing scheduled for March 18, 2026
2. Facilities team to finalize Greenville lease terms by March 28
3. HR to prepare retention and relocation packages for affected staff by April 4
4. IT to deliver infrastructure migration plan by April 11
5. Legal review of Charlotte lease termination options by March 21
attachments:
1. Prologis Site Evaluation Report — Greenville, SC (January 2026)
2. Financial Model — Consolidation Scenarios A, B, and C
3. Greenville Facility Floor Plan and Logistics Map
4. Workforce Transition Impact Assessment
